exploration_log.aha_moment: The args slice after index n is exactly the unexpected arguments; args[n:] captures them cleanly with no off-by-one issues.
exploration_log.hindsight: Could have also listed ALL received args (not just extra) for a more informative message, but the task specifically says 'unexpected argument values'.

alternatives: Always include extra args in both cases (too-few has none)
Include all args in both error messages
key_decisions.decision: Split the too-many-args case from the too-few-args case and only add extra arg listing for too-many
key_decisions.reasoning: The task specifically says 'extra arguments' in the too-many-args case; too-few args has nothing to list

alternatives: Change test helper to accept expected string parameter
key_decisions.decision: Use strings.HasPrefix + strings.Contains in test helper instead of per-test exact strings
key_decisions.reasoning: The helper is shared across 5 tests with different extra arg values; generic prefix/contains check is cleaner

solve_notes: Added a branch in `ExactArgs` to detect the too-many-args case (len > n) and include the extra argument values via `strings.Join(args[n:], ", ")` in the error message. Updated the test helper `exactArgsWithInvalidCount` to use prefix/contains checks instead of exact equality, since the new message format varies by the actual extra args.
task_text:
Fix ExactArgs validator not producing a clear error when too many arguments are given

When `ExactArgs(2)` is used and 5 arguments are supplied, the error
message says "accepts 2 arg(s), received 5" without listing the extra
arguments. Fix the `ExactArgs` validator in `args.go` to include the
unexpected argument values in the error message, making debugging easier
for end users.

relevant_preexisting_tests.reason: Calls ExactArgs(2) with 3 args, directly exercises the too-many-args error path
Calls ExactArgs(2) with 3 valid args, same error path
Calls ExactValidArgs(2) which delegates to ExactArgs(2) with 3 args

test_selection.test_query: Find tests that verify the behavior of ExactArgs in args.go

thrash_preventing_defs.name: exactArgsWithInvalidCount
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: this helper has an exact string match for the old error message; must update it to avoid test failures when changing the error format

tier_difference_reasoning: minimum_sufficient_defs is just ExactArgs since that's the only production change. thrash_preventing adds the test helper that an AI agent would search for to avoid breaking tests — without seeing it upfront, the agent would implement the fix, run tests, see failures, then have to backtrack to find and update the helper.
